mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate compresses the risk-free discount rate and reduces corporate borrowing costs, which mechanically increases the present value of future equity cash flows and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods in the bottom quartile of FEDFUNDS fail to produce statistically higher positive 20-day returns than top-quartile periods over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.5 percent, it triggers a downward repricing of corporate earnings expectations due to anticipated demand destruction and operating leverage compression, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: More than 50 percent of instances with UNRATE above 5.5 percent are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1,100 thousand units signal a contraction in residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Observations with HOUST below 1,100 are followed by negative 60-day returns in fewer than 45 percent of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-interest-rate regime, a rising unemployment rate indicates labor market scarring that is not offset by conventional monetary easing, causing equity risk premia to expand and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.25 percent, increases in UNRATE fail to predict negative 60-day returns with an information coefficient below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales captures a rebound in consumer spending velocity that is quickly reflected in equity prices, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in RSAFS are followed by positive 5-day returns in less than 55 percent of occurrences during out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production indicate sustained manufacturing contraction and inventory destocking, which reduces corporate revenue growth and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Two consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO are followed by negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in payroll employment and retail sales indicate a coordinated demand expansion across income generation and spending, producing stronger positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days than either signal in isolation.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS and RSAFS both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS and RSAFS increases does not outperform the best single-series signal by at least 5 percentage points in 60-day hit rate over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month consumer price inflation acceleration when the policy rate is constrained at the effective lower bound erodes real corporate margins and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of positive CPIAUCSL change with FEDFUNDS below 0.25 percent fails to produce negative 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances.
